prompt: The generic class cannot be instantiated with primitives. What is the bug?
choices: Java generics only work with reference types; int is a primitive and cannot be used as a type argument; use Box<Integer> instead
The constructor Box(T v) must explicitly cast v to int
Generic classes must extend Number to hold numeric values
explanation: Java generics use type erasure and compile to Object internally, so type arguments must be reference types. int is a primitive and is not allowed as a type argument — Box<int> is a compile error. Fix: use Box<Integer>. Autoboxing handles the conversion automatically: new Box<>(5) works with Box<Integer>.
